name: proto2: required, optional, and default values
info:
This flow encodes and decodes a `Proto2Type` message defined with `syntax = "proto2"`.

proto2 features shown in `examples/protos/proto2.proto`:

- `required string name` - encoding fails if it is missing. Try removing `name` from the inject payload and watch the encode node raise an error.
- `optional` fields with `[default = ...]` - decoded messages fall back to the declared default when the field was not sent.
- `repeated string tags` - a list field.

The protobuf-file config node points at:

    node_modules/@pauldeng/node-red-contrib-protobuf/examples/protos/proto2.proto

This relative path works when Node-RED runs from your user directory (for example `~/.node-red`). If the proto file fails to load, open the protobuf-file config node and enter the absolute path on your machine.
